input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Evaluation point outside the training envelope.

case_context.cou: linear surrogate evaluated 20% beyond the training load, within the linear regime
case_context.decision_consequence: design decision at the evaluation load

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Although the load is outside the training box, the response is provably linear-elastic over the full range (well below yield) and the linearity is documented, so a validated linear surrogate is applicable by construction at the point — the extrapolation does not cross a regime boundary.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: response is verified linear-elastic over the range; documented linearity bound covers the point

forbidden_claims: demands re-validation despite a documented linearity bound covering the point (over-action)
treats the numeric box boundary as the applicability limit when physics bounds the regime

hardness.obvious_signal: the evaluation load is 20% above the maximum training load — clear extrapolation
hardness.conflicting_signal: the response is provably LINEAR-elastic over the whole range (verified, well below yield), so a validated linear surrogate extrapolates exactly within the regime, and the package documents the linearity bound covering the point

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 applicability: the validated domain is bounded by physics + evidence, not a numeric box; a linear surrogate within a verified linear regime is applicable.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
